description: You are a reliability engineer performing a Weibull reliability analysis on accelerated life testing (ALT) data for Class-H motor insulation located at /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/motor_insulation_life.csv. Predict the B10 life at the normal operating temperature of 180°C using an Arrhenius-Weibull model based on test data from 40 specimens at 190°C, 220°C, 240°C, and 260°C. Fit a 2-parameter Weibull distribution to the data at each test temperature, accounting for censored data. Save the estimated parameters (temperature_C, weibull_shape, weibull_scale, B10_life, B50_life) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/insulation_weibull_params.csv. Fit an Arrhenius-Weibull acceleration model to extrapolate to 180°C and save the prediction (temperature_C, predicted_B10_hours, weibull_scale_predicted) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/insulation_reliability_prediction.csv. Generate a multi-panel plot showing Weibull probability plots and the Arrhenius relationship at /home/ga/RProjects/output/insulation_analysis_plots.png. Write the analysis script in /home/ga/RProjects/insulation_analysis.R.
